name: tail_system_logs
description:
Reads the last N lines of a local log file — ideal for debugging runtime errors from dev servers, npm run dev, uvicorn, or any process that writes to a file.
Use when: a process errored and you need to see recent log output, you're tailing a dev server log, or you want to check system/application logs without leaving the agent session.
Scope: local filesystem only. Read-only. Any file the current user can read.
Not for: streaming live log output (returns a snapshot), checking if a process is running (use list_processes), or reading structured log files as JSON (returns raw lines).

path.description: Absolute path to the log file. Example: '/var/log/nginx/error.log' or '/tmp/dev-server.log'.

lines.description: Number of lines to read from the end of the file. Default: 50. Max: 1000.

filter.description: Optional: only return lines containing this string (case-insensitive). Useful for filtering 'error', 'warn', 'exception'.

encoding.description: File encoding. Default: 'utf-8'. Use 'latin-1' for legacy log files with non-UTF characters.
